original: First appearance of Pokémon in the role-playing video game "Pocket Monsters Red and Green" for Game Boy in Japan
Biggest cosmic explosion ever detected from a supermassive black hole in Ophiuchus galaxy 390 million lights years away, (size of 15 Miky Ways), published in "Astrophysical Journal"
Smallest baby boy ever born to be later successfully released, goes home, after original birth weight of 268g (9.45oz) from Tokyo hospital
Greatest passenger load on a commercial airliner-610 on Boeing 747
Doctors announce world's second known case of semi-identical twins, a boy and girl from Brisbane, Australia
The government of Italy asks for help to keep the Leaning Tower of Pisa from toppling over.
Charles Best, American-Canadian medicalscientistand co-discoverer of insulin, born in West Pembroke, Maine (d. 1978)
Martin Kamen and Sam Ruben discover carbon-14 (radiocarbon dating) at the University of California Radiation Laboratory in Berkeley, California
8.8 magnitude earthquake rattles central Chile; epicenter just off the coast also triggers a tsunami
Borley Rectory, "the most haunted house in England", destroyed in a fire
Chaos erupts after the Swedish Public Employment Service mistakenly invites 61,000 people to a job interview in Stockholm
Russian-American chemist Constantin Fahlberg discovers an artificial sweetener (later named 'saccharin'), while working on coal tar analysis at Johns Hopkins University in Baltimore, Maryland
Kelly Johnson, American aircraft engineer (Lockheed Skunk Works, Lockheed U-2, SR-71 Blackbird), born in Ishpeming, Michigan (d. 1990)
US Olympic Ice Hockey Team beats USSR 3-2 en route to gold medal
Britain's House of Lords agrees to end 1,000 years of male precedence by giving a monarch's first-born daughter the same claim to the throne as any first born son
First Mardi Gras celebration in New Orleans
Charlotte Ray, 1st African American woman lawyer in USA, graduates from Howard University
US Supreme Court unanimously upholds 19th amendment to the US Constuituent - women's right to vote
Bonnie Blair(US) wins Olympic 500m speed skating in record 39.1
Carl Lewisjumps world record indoor (8.675 m)
Swedish pop groupABBAarrives in Australia for the first time, sparking "ABBA-mania"
Mary-Claire King, American human geneticist (breast cancer genes), born in Evanston, Illinois
David H. Hubel, Canadian neuroscientist, 1981 Nobel Prize for "discoveries concerning information processing in the visual system", born in Windsor, Ontario (d. 2013)[1]
Alejandro Zaffaroni, Uruguayan-born biochemist, helped create birth control pill and nicotine patch, born in Montevideo, Uruguay (d. 2014)[1]
Alice Hamilton, American physician, scientist and social advocate (workmen's compensation laws, occupational illnesses), born in Manhattan, New York (d. 1970)[1]
Radio emissions from the Sun first detected by English physicist and radio astronomer James Stanley Hey, working on WWII radar anti-jamming  methods
15th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Dutch swimmer Willy den Ouden sets new women's world 100m freestyle record (1:04.6) in Amsterdam; lasts 22 years until broken in 1956 by Dawn Fraser of Australia
Boston CelticBob Cousysets NBA record with 28 assists Boston Celtics score 173 points against Minneapolis Lakers
Australian cricketerDon Bradmanscores 169 in the 5th Test match against England in 223 minutes, ensuring Australia wins the Ashes 3-2
Dan Issel (NBA-Nuggets), hits on 63rd consecutive free throw
Paul McCartneyand Stevie Wonder record the single "Ebony & Ivory"
"Captain Marvel" film directed by Anna Boden and Ryan Fleck, starringBrie LarsonandSamuel L. Jacksonpremieres
Neal Schon, American rock guitarist and songwriter (Santana, 1971-72; Journey - "Don't Stop Believin'"; "Any Way You Want It"; Bad English, 1987-91), born in Tinker Air Force Base, Oklahoma
Adrian Smith, English heavy metal guitarist (Iron Maiden - "Aces High"), born in London Borough of Hackney, London, England
1st public performance of Holst's "Planets"
N. Scott Momaday, Native American author (House Made of Dawn, Pulitzer Prize - 1969), born in Lawton, Oklahoma (d. 2024)[1]
Dexter Gordon, American jazz tenor saxophonist (Go!; The Other Side of 'Round Midnight), and actor ('Round Midnight), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 1990)

Veikko Hakulinen of Finland wins his 3rd career Olympic cross country gold medal as part of Finland's 4 x 10k relay team at Squaw Valley, CA; winner: 50k (Oslo, 1952) and 30k (Cortina d’Ampezzo, 1956)
Mickey Mantleof the NY Yankees signs a baseball contract worth $100,000
Test Cricket debut ofClarrie Grimmett, who took 5-45 & 6-37 v England
Abraham Lincolnmakes a speech at Cooper Union in the city of New York that is largely responsible for his election to the Presidency
Ludwig van Beethoven's Symphony No. 8 in F major premieres in Vienna
German composerRobert Schumannsaved from suicide attempt in Rhine River
English explorerWilliam Dampieris the first British person to visit the Pacific Island of New Britain, which he names
Baseball 1st played in England at Lord's Cricket Ground
Dominican Republic gains independence from Haiti (National Day)
Dominica gains independence from England
The flag of Argentina, created byManuel Belgrano, is first raised at Rosario
Henry IVcrowned King of France at Chartres Cathedral, only French monarch to be crowned there (Reins and Paris occupied by the Catholic League)
Israel & Egypt exchange ambassadors
Rio de la Plata Treaty
Oil pipe line from Rotterdam to Ruhrgebied opens
Dutch socialist Samuel van Wooden demands law against child labor
Bernard Lyot, French astronomer (Coronagraph, Lyot-filter), born in Paris (d. 1952)
Antoinette Sibley, British ballerina (Turning Point), born in Bromley, England
Alfred Einhorn, German chemist (created Novocain), born in Hamburg, Germany (d. 1917)[1]
Constantine Mavrocordatos, Greek reforming Prince of Wallachia and Prince of Moldavia who abolished serfdom, born in Constantinople, Ottoman Empire (d. 1769)
Angelina Weld Grimke, American journalist, teacher and playwright (Harlem Renaissance), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1958)
Bertus Brouwers, Dutch mathematician and philosopher (founder of modern topology), born in Overschie, Netherlands (d. 1966)
